nodes.description: Progressive accumulation of unremodeled bone obliterates the medullary cavity, causing loss of hematopoietic marrow space with resultant pancytopenia. Compensatory extramedullary hematopoiesis in liver and spleen causes hepatosplenomegaly.

nodes.description: Bone pain is a very frequent symptom in autosomal recessive malignant osteopetrosis, reflecting the abnormal bone microarchitecture, microfractures, and periosteal reactions from progressive sclerosis.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002653 | Bone pain | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Progressive skull thickening narrows cranial nerve foramina, causing optic nerve compression (blindness), facial nerve palsy, and sensorineural hearing loss.

nodes.description: Premature fusion of cranial sutures occurs in malignant infantile osteopetrosis as a consequence of the pathologically dense, sclerotic skull bone, contributing to raised intracranial pressure and neurological complications.

nodes.description: Poor growth and nutritional failure are part of the common early presentation of severe autosomal recessive osteopetrosis.

nodes.description: Growth delay is a very frequent feature of malignant infantile osteopetrosis, reflecting chronic illness, metabolic derangements, and impaired endochondral ossification.

nodes.description: Despite markedly increased bone density, the bone microarchitecture is disorganized and brittle, leading to recurrent pathologic fractures. This paradox of dense but fragile bone is a hallmark of malignant infantile osteopetrosis.

nodes.description: TCIRG1 encodes the a3 subunit of the vacuolar H+-ATPase (V-ATPase), which is specifically expressed in osteoclasts and essential for proton secretion into the resorption lacuna. Loss-of-function mutations abolish osteoclast acidification capacity, preventing dissolution of hydroxyapatite mineral in bone matrix. Osteoclasts differentiate normally and are present in normal or elevated numbers but cannot resorb bone.
nodes.id: Vacuolar Proton Pump Deficiency in Osteoclasts
